commentary_text: The model assigns Croatia a 46% win probability while the sportsbook consensus reaches 64%, a 18-percentage-point gap favoring the market's confidence in a Croatia victory. The market also narrows Panama's chances to 14% against the model's 23%, an 9-percentage-point divergence in the opposite direction. Both sources rank Croatia as favorite, but the market has substantially tightened the contest in Croatia's favor. This sharpening could reflect recent lineup news, injury updates, or current form information unavailable to the model's last training cycle. The dashboard surfaces this asymmetry as a signal of where real-money flows are concentrating conviction, without asserting which probability better reflects the true match outcome.
